---
title: "Is Singapore's Healthcare Adequate for its Ageing Population?"
subtitle: "IS630 Statistical Thinking in Data Science · G1 Team 3"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    controls: true
    transition: fade
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.08
    self-contained: true
    highlight-style: github
execute:
  echo: false
---

## {.title-slide-custom background-color="#065A82"}

::: {style="text-align:center; padding-top: 60px;"}
[Is Singapore's Healthcare]{style="font-size:2.4rem; font-weight:700; color:#ffffff; display:block;"}
[Adequate for its Ageing Population?]{style="font-size:2.4rem; font-weight:700; color:#7ECBF5; display:block; margin-bottom:24px;"}

[IS630 Statistical Thinking in Data Science]{style="font-size:1.1rem; color:rgba(255,255,255,0.75);"}

::: {style="margin-top:32px; display:flex; justify-content:center; gap:12px; flex-wrap:wrap;"}
[Cheng Ho Wang]{.team-pill} [Hong Huajie]{.team-pill} [Teo Cher In]{.team-pill} [Wu Jiayan]{.team-pill} [Ye Zhili]{.team-pill} [Yee Weicong Mark]{.team-pill}
:::
[G1 Team 3]{style="font-size:0.9rem; color:rgba(255,255,255,0.5); margin-top:16px; display:block;"}
:::

---

## Agenda {background-color="#f0f7fb"}

::: {style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:20px; padding-top:20px;"}

::: {.agenda-card}
[01]{.agenda-num} **Introduction**
Global ageing phenomenon & context
:::

::: {.agenda-card}
[02]{.agenda-num} **Study Objective**
Key research questions
:::

::: {.agenda-card}
[03]{.agenda-num} **Data**
Sources & cleaning methodology
:::

::: {.agenda-card}
[04]{.agenda-num} **Summary Statistics**
Population & hospital beds overview
:::

::: {.agenda-card}
[05]{.agenda-num} **Inferential Statistics**
Correlation, hypothesis, forecasting
:::

::: {.agenda-card}
[06]{.agenda-num} **Results & Conclusion**
Findings & recommendations
:::

:::

---

## Introduction {background-color="#ffffff"}

::: {style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:48px; align-items:center; padding-top:10px;"}

::: {}
[Population ageing is a **global phenomenon**.]{style="font-size:1.15rem; font-weight:600; color:#065A82;"}

By **2080**, the elderly (aged 65+) is expected to **outnumber children** (under 18) worldwide.

Singapore is at the forefront of this demographic shift, creating urgent pressure on healthcare infrastructure.
:::

::: {style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:16px;"}
::: {.impact-card style="border-color:#065A82;"}
**Economic Impact**
Workforce contraction, rising pension costs, productivity challenges
:::
::: {.impact-card style="border-color:#1C7293;"}
**Healthcare Impact**
Surge in chronic disease burden, demand for long-term care
:::
::: {.impact-card style="border-color:#21295C;"}
**Societal Impact**
Policy reforms needed for demographic transition
:::
:::

:::

---

## Study Objective {background-color="#f0f7fb"}

::: {style="text-align:center; padding-bottom:28px;"}
[Through data analysis, we aim to gain insights into the state of Singapore's healthcare infrastructure and its ability to support the future needs of its population.]{style="font-size:1.05rem; color:#444; font-style:italic; max-width:800px; display:inline-block;"}
:::

::: {style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:24px;"}

::: {.question-card}
**Question 1**

What is the **impact of age** on healthcare demand?

*Examining admission rates and chronic illness burden by age group*
:::

::: {.question-card}
**Question 2**

Will there be **enough hospital beds** by 2030?

*Forecasting bed supply vs. projected elderly admissions*
:::

:::

---

## Data {background-color="#ffffff"}

::: {style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:40px; align-items:start; padding-top:10px;"}

::: {}
### Sources

::: {.source-badge}
🏛️ **Singapore Government**
Official health statistics, MOH data, population census
:::

::: {.source-badge style="margin-top:12px;"}
📊 **Statista**
Supplementary demographic & healthcare metrics
:::
:::

::: {}
### Cleaning Pipeline

::: {style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:12px;"}
::: {.step-row}
[1]{.step-num} Combined all data sources into a single unified file
:::
::: {.step-row}
[2]{.step-num} Addressed null values and data inconsistencies
:::
::: {.step-row}
[3]{.step-num} Aggregated data for analysis — elderly sub-groups, bed categories
:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Summary Statistics · Ageing Population {background-color="#f0f7fb"}

::: {style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr); gap:16px; margin-bottom:24px;"}
::: {.stat-box}
[7.2%]{.stat-num} Elderly share (2000)
:::
::: {.stat-box}
[18%]{.stat-num} Elderly share (2024)
:::
::: {.stat-box}
[83.5 yrs]{.stat-num} Life expectancy (2024)
:::
::: {.stat-box}
[↓]{.stat-num} Fertility & birth rate
:::
:::

::: {style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:24px;"}

::: {.insight-block}
**Population Age Profile**

Negative population age graph reflecting low fertility. Middle-aged cohort forms the largest resident share. Rapidly increasing proportion of residents aged 65+.
:::

::: {.insight-block}
**Outlook**

With steady life expectancy growth reaching **83.5 years** in 2024, a continuous rise in the elderly population proportion is expected through 2030 and beyond.
:::

:::

---

## Summary Statistics · Hospital Beds {background-color="#ffffff"}

::: {style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:40px;"}

::: {}
### Overall Bed Supply

::: {.metric-row}
[+74.22%]{style="color:#065A82; font-weight:700;"} total increase since baseline
:::
::: {.metric-row}
[+9.46%]{style="color:#1C7293; font-weight:700;"} maximum single-year growth (2015)
:::

**Nursing home beds** now exceed **50% share** — the largest single bed category.

**Community hospital beds** show the fastest growth at **+198.7%**.
:::

::: {}
### Admissions per Bed

::: {.insight-block}
A **surge in acute admission demand** among those aged 65+ reflects the growing healthcare intensity of the elderly cohort.
:::

::: {.insight-block}
**Community hospitals remain relatively stable**, suggesting they are better sized for current demand — but forecasts raise concern.
:::
:::

:::

---

## Inferential Statistics · Correlation Analysis {background-color="#f0f7fb"}

::: {style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:32px; padding-top:10px;"}

::: {}
### What We Tested

Pearson correlation between:

- Elderly population size and hospital bed demand
- Age-group proportions and admission rates
- Healthcare expenditure and population ageing

### Significance Testing

Correlation Significance Test (Pearson) applied to validate the strength of observed relationships.
:::

::: {}
### Key Insight

::: {.highlight-box}
Strong positive correlations were found between **elderly population growth** and **acute bed admissions**, confirming age as a primary driver of healthcare demand.
:::

Regression coefficient significance tests confirmed that ageing population metrics are **statistically significant** predictors of bed utilisation.
:::

:::

---

## Inferential Statistics · Forecasting {background-color="#ffffff"}

::: {style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:32px; padding-top:10px;"}

::: {}
### Elderly Population by 2030

::: {.forecast-card}
[+36%]{style="font-size:2.2rem; font-weight:700; color:#065A82;"} increase in ageing population admission rate

**1,026,282** elderly residents projected by 2030
:::

*Assumption: Proportion of beds for the elderly reflects admission rate — 73.8% of acute beds and 97.1% of community beds.*
:::

::: {}
### Hospital Beds by 2030

::: {style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:12px;"}
::: {.forecast-card}
**Acute beds:** +15.8% → **13,924 beds**
:::
::: {.forecast-card}
**Community beds:** +29.1% → **2,973 beds**
:::
::: {.forecast-card style="background:#fff3cd; border-color:#e6a817;"}
**⚠️ Shortfall of ~6,800 beds**
Current growth rate insufficient
:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Results {background-color="#f0f7fb"}

::: {style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr); gap:20px; margin-bottom:28px;"}
::: {.result-card}
📈 **Increased Life Expectancy**
Drives sustained growth in elderly population
:::
::: {.result-card}
📉 **Lower Fertility & Birth Rate**
Negative population age pyramid
:::
::: {.result-card}
💰 **Increased Healthcare Expenditure**
Projected SGD 30B by 2030
:::
:::

::: {style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:20px;"}
::: {.result-card}
🏥 **Higher Hospital Admissions**
Surge in acute demand among 65+
:::
::: {.result-card style="background:#fff3cd; border-color:#e6a817;"}
⚠️ **~6,800 Bed Shortfall by 2030**
MOH's +4,000 beds plan falls short of projected need
:::
:::

---

## Conclusion {background-color="#065A82"}

::: {style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:24px; padding-top:16px;"}

::: {.concl-card}
[🟢]{style="font-size:1.5rem;"} **Near-Term Readiness**
Singapore's healthcare system can meet ageing population needs in the near term
:::

::: {.concl-card}
[🔴]{style="font-size:1.5rem;"} **Long-Term Limits**
Expansion is constrained by land and manpower shortages, even with higher spending
:::

::: {.concl-card}
[💪]{style="font-size:1.5rem;"} **Healthy Ageing**
Encouraging healthy ageing reduces pressure on healthcare services
:::

::: {.concl-card}
[🏛️]{style="font-size:1.5rem;"} **Government Measures**
Job support, economic sustainability, and retirement policies complement healthcare reform
:::

:::

::: {style="text-align:center; margin-top:24px; color:rgba(255,255,255,0.7); font-size:0.9rem;"}
*More research is needed to assess the true effectiveness of Singapore's overall ageing strategy.*
:::
